problem_statement: Knowledge base search is using prefix-only matching, so articles are returned only when the query appears at the start of the topic, title, or content field. Matches in the middle of those fields are missed.
goal: Change knowledge base search to use substring/contains matching so any article is returned when the query appears anywhere in topic, title, or content.
acceptance_criteria: A search term appearing mid-sentence in an article's content returns that article.
Search still returns articles for prefix matches.
Search covers topic, title, and content fields consistently.
constraints: Keep the change localized to the backend search implementation.
unknowns: Whether search is implemented with Python string checks or a database query.
Whether search normalization includes case folding or punctuation handling.
repo_summary: Backend repository with a dedicated knowledge service, API route, model, and schema for knowledge articles. The issue explicitly points to backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py as the affected area.

targeted_symbols.reason: Most likely contains the prefix-only matching logic and is the smallest relevant implementation area named in the issue.

localization_rationale: The symptom clearly indicates prefix matching, which is typically implemented in a search function using startswith or a leading-anchored SQL LIKE.
The issue explicitly identifies backend/app/services/knowledge_service.py (search) as the affected file, so localization is strong and narrow.
Route/model/schema files are likely just plumbing; the actual matching logic should live in the service layer.
